articles.author: Omar Sohail
articles.title: Exynos 2600 Was Born Out Of Sheer Necessity And Wouldn’t Have Entered Mass Production If Samsung’s Mistakes Not Cost It $3 Billion Last Year - Wccftech
articles.description: As Samsung races towards self-sufficiency in the chipset race, it only mass produced the Exynos 2600 because it lost $3 billion in 2025
articles.content: The race to achieve chipset autonomy for Samsung began with the Exynos 2600, which is found in various Galaxy S26 and Galaxy S26+ models, and is the companys first 2nm GAA SoC. Needless to say, the K… [+2405 chars]

articles.author: Ryan Whitwam
articles.title: Google shoehorned Rust into Pixel 10 modem to make legacy code safer - Ars Technica
articles.description: Cellular modems are complex black boxes of legacy code, but Google is making them safer with Rust.
